notes: Confirms the fix — prev_skill (broken ✅) still fails; with_skill (corrected ✅) passes
NOTE: 'without_skill' = previous skill version (rules present, broken ✅ counter-example at 76 chars). Not a zero-skill baseline.
with_skill achieves 100% pass rate across all 3 evals — first perfect score in this eval set.
The counter-example fix was the only change; it resolved the eval-3 failure cleanly (76→65 char subject).
eval-1 now passes 5/5 in both configurations — the rules + body requirement from example 2 body appear to prompt the model to include a body even for simple fixes.
eval-2 remains non-discriminating (both 100%). Should be replaced with a harder case in the next iteration.
with_skill is also faster (-21.5s mean) and cheaper (-1478 tokens) than prev_skill — likely because prev_skill spent more time on eval-3 (142s vs 72s).
